engineGuide.h2: Nissan Skyline Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan Skyline across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level turbo RB engine for R32 GTS-t, offering 212bhp with proven reliability and lighter weight than larger RB units.
compatibleVariants: R32 GTS-t, R32 GTS-t Type M
commonFailures: Crank angle sensor failure causing misfire at 2500-3500rpm
Oil pressure sensor giving false low readings
Turbo seal wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB20DET Nissan Skyline engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: The most common Skyline turbo engine, powering R33/R34 GT-T models with 250-276bhp and strong aftermarket support.
compatibleVariants: R33 GT-T, R33 GTS25t, R34 GT-T, R34 25GT Turbo
commonFailures: CAS (crank angle sensor) failure causing timing errors and misfire
Individual coil pack failure leading to cylinder misfire
Oil pressure sensor failure causing false low gauge readings
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB25DET Nissan Skyline engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: R34-exclusive NEO variant with VVT, stronger bottom end and 276bhp output - the most desirable RB25 for performance builds.
commonFailures: VVT solenoid issues affecting variable timing operation
CAS failure causing intermittent misfire under load
Oil pressure sensor giving false low readings
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB25DET NEO Nissan Skyline engine replacement

entries.title: 2.6L Petrol
entries.history: The legendary GT-R twin-turbo straight-6, officially 276bhp but actually ~320bhp, defining JDM performance culture worldwide.
compatibleVariants: R32 GT-R, R33 GT-R, R34 GT-R, R34 GT-R V-Spec, R34 GT-R Nür
commonFailures: Oil pump drive collar failure on early R32/R33 causing catastrophic oil starvation
Factory ceramic turbo wheel disintegration above 0.9-1.0 bar boost
Timing belt failure (interference engine) if not replaced every 100,000km
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB26DETT Nissan Skyline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth 3.5L V6 powering V35 Skyline 350GT and Infiniti G35, offering refined performance with 280bhp.
commonFailures: Oil consumption issues on higher mileage units
Gallery gasket failure causing oil leaks
Throttle body carbon buildup affecting idle quality
entries.cta: Get quotes for VQ35DE Nissan Skyline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: High-revving evolution of the VQ35 with dual intake systems, 311bhp output and improved responsiveness for V36 models.
commonFailures: Light throttle misfire between 3000-3200rpm (throttle body/MAF related)
Gallery gasket failure causing oil pressure issues
Variable valve timing solenoid wear affecting performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for VQ35HR Nissan Skyline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol
entries.history: Hand-built 3.8L twin-turbo V6 exclusive to R35 GT-R, producing 480-600bhp and representing Nissan's modern performance flagship.
compatibleVariants: R35 GT-R, R35 GT-R Premium, R35 GT-R NISMO
commonFailures: Turbo actuator failure causing boost control issues

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear on early units
entries.cta: Get quotes for VR38DETT Nissan Skyline engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Skyline Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: RB26DETT Oil Pump Drive Failure - Why Early R32/R33 GT-R Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Skyline R32 GT-R, R33 GT-R (1989-1998, RB26DETT)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-100,000 miles under high G-loading or track use
problems.rootCause: Early RB26 oil pump drive collar is poorly designed and can chew the crankshaft nose under high RPM or lateral loads, causing catastrophic oil starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install aftermarket crank collar kit before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents oil pump drive failure; essential for any RB26 rebuild or track use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded oil pump drive, forged internals optional
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full performance; forged builds support 600bhp+; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: If you own an early R32 or R33 GT-R, the crank collar fix is essential preventative maintenance. If oil pressure has dropped or you hear bottom-end knock, replacement is the only safe option. Forged RB26 builds hold value well for performance owners.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt RB26DETT engine prices for your Nissan Skyline GT-R

problems.h4: RB25DET CAS Timing Failure - Why Skyline GT-T Engines Misfire Under Load
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Skyline R33 GT-T, R34 GT-T (1995-2002, RB25DET / RB25DET NEO)
problems.rootCause: The crank angle sensor (CAS) fails internally, causing intermittent timing errors that manifest as misfire between 2500-3500rpm, especially under boost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty crank angle sensor with OEM or upgraded aftermarket unit
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves misfire if CAS is the sole cause; typical lifespan 60k-100k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace CAS, all coil packs, spark plugs and ignition leads
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses multiple potential misfire causes; recommended for high-mileage RB25 units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new CAS, coils, timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear; includes 12-month warranty and upgraded internals

problems.recommendation: Start with CAS diagnosis - it's the most common culprit. If misfire persists after CAS and coil replacement, or if the engine has high mileage, a rebuilt RB25DET often represents better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for RB25DET Nissan Skyline engine replacement from UK specialists

problems.h4: RB26DETT Ceramic Turbo Failure - Why Boost Over 1.0 Bar Risks Engine Damage
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage if boost exceeds 0.9-1.0 bar on stock turbos
problems.rootCause: Factory ceramic exhaust turbine wheels are brittle and can shatter under excessive boost or heat, with fragments potentially sucked back into the engine causing catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace ceramic turbos with steel-wheel aftermarket units
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents ceramic failure; essential for any boost increase beyond stock

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbos, inspect engine for ceramic fragment damage, clean oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Required if ceramic failure suspected; prevents secondary damage from debris

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with steel-wheel turbos, upgraded oil pump drive
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if ceramic fragments entered engine; forged builds support higher boost safely

problems.recommendation: Never run increased boost on stock ceramic turbos. If you hear turbo whistle or see smoke on boost, stop driving immediately and inspect. For any performance build, steel-wheel turbos and a rebuilt RB26 with forged internals is the only safe path.
problems.cta: Compare forged RB26DETT engine prices for your Nissan Skyline GT-R

problems.h4: VQ35HR Light Throttle Misfire - Why V36 Skyline 350GT Hesitates at 3000rpm
problems.rootCause: Throttle body carbon buildup or MAF sensor contamination causes lean condition at light throttle, triggering misfire codes between 3000-3200rpm.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean throttle body, replace MAF sensor, perform ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves misfire if caused by carbon/MAF issues; recommended first step

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace variable valve timing solenoids, update ECU software
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses VVT-related misfire; common on higher-mileage VQ35HR units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new VVT components, updated gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if engine has gallery gasket failure or high oil consumption

problems.recommendation: Start with throttle body cleaning and MAF replacement - this resolves most VQ35HR misfire cases. If symptoms persist or oil consumption is high, a rebuilt VQ35HR with updated gallery gaskets offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for VQ35HR Nissan Skyline engine replacement from UK specialists

problems.h4: RB25DET Oil Pressure Sensor Failure - Why Your Skyline Gauge Reads Low
problems.rootCause: The oil pressure sending unit fails internally, causing the gauge to read low or zero even when actual oil pressure is normal, leading to unnecessary panic and engine replacement enquiries.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pressure sending unit with OEM or upgraded unit
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves false low reading; always verify actual pressure with mechanical gauge first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace sensor, check oil pump pickup, inspect for sludge buildup
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if sensor failure suspected alongside other oil-related symptoms

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new oil pump, pickup, and sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if actual oil pressure is low; sensor failure alone doesn't require replacement

problems.recommendation: Always verify actual oil pressure with a mechanical gauge before condemning the engine. Most "low oil pressure" RB25 cases are just sensor failure - a £100 fix, not a £4,000 replacement.
problems.cta: Compare RB25DET Nissan Skyline engine prices - verify before you replace

problems.h4: VR38DETT Turbo Actuator Failure - Why R35 GT-R Loses Boost Control
problems.rootCause: Electronic turbo actuators wear or fail, causing inconsistent boost control, limp mode, and reduced performance - a known issue on early R35 units.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty turbo actuator(s), recalibrate boost control
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves boost control issues; typical lifespan 40k-80k miles on early units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Service both turbos, replace actuators, perform full ECU calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage R35 units or track-use vehicles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo actuators, forged internals optional
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if actuator failure caused secondary engine damage; forged builds support higher power

problems.recommendation: Actuator replacement is usually sufficient for boost control issues. For track use or performance builds, consider a rebuilt VR38 with upgraded actuators and forged internals to handle increased stress.
problems.cta: Get quotes for VR38DETT Nissan Skyline engine replacement from UK R35 specialists

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Skyline
finalCta.paragraph: From RB26 oil pump failures to VQ35HR misfire patterns, Nissan Skyline engines have known weak points - but replacement doesn't mean dealer prices. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices from multiple suppliers in minutes and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Skyline Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Skyline and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Nissan Skyline Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Nissan Skyline engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Nissan Skyline Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally stripped, inspected and rebuilt to factory spec with new bearings, seals and timing components.
types.frontDescription: Professionally stripped, inspected and rebuilt to factory spec with new bearings, seals and timing components.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code, RB26DETT at premium end.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned units undergo full disassembly, machining to factory tolerances and reassembly with new wear components. Ideal for RB25DET, VQ35HR and standard RB26DETT rebuilds.
backBullets: Includes new bearings, seals, gaskets and timing belt/chain
Best value for GT-T and 350GT owners seeking reliability
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Nissan Skyline engine prices

types.title: Nissan Skyline Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded internals addressing known failure points like RB26 oil pump drive.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded internals addressing known failure points like RB26 oil pump drive.
types.frontDisclaimer: Forged RB26DETT builds at upper end of range.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines feature upgraded components targeting model-specific weaknesses - crank collars for RB26, VVT solenoids for VQ35HR, steel turbos for ceramic-prone units.
backBullets: Customisable for performance: forged pistons/rods available
Includes upgraded oil pump drive, CAS, sensors as needed
Preferred choice for GT-R owners and track-focused builds

types.title: Nissan Skyline Used Engines

types.frontDisclaimer: Verify ceramic turbo condition on RB26DETT units.
types.backDescription: Used engines offer cost-effective replacement when budget is tight. Always request compression test results, oil pressure readings and service history before purchase.

Ideal for GTS-t, GT-T and V35 owners on a budget
Always budget for ancillary replacement: sensors, belts, fluids

items.title: Nissan Skyline 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The RB20DET powered the entry-level R32 GTS-t, offering 212bhp with proven turbo reliability and lighter weight than larger RB units - ideal for balanced handling and affordable performance builds.
commonFailurePoints: Crank angle sensor failure causing misfire at 2500-3500rpm; oil pressure sensor giving false low readings

items.title: Nissan Skyline 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The RB25DET family powered R33 and R34 GT-T models, delivering 250-276bhp with strong aftermarket support; the NEO variant added VVT and a stronger bottom end for R34 exclusives.
commonFailurePoints: CAS timing failure causing misfire under load; coil pack failure; oil pressure sensor failure
items.cta: Compare Nissan Skyline 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Skyline 2.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary RB26DETT twin-turbo straight-6 powered all R32/R33/R34 GT-R models, officially rated at 276bhp but actually producing ~320bhp - the defining JDM performance engine.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump drive collar failure on early R32/R33; ceramic turbo wheel disintegration above 0.9-1.0 bar boost

items.title: Nissan Skyline 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The VQ35 family powered V35 and V36 Skyline 350GT models, offering refined V6 performance with 280-311bhp; the HR variant added high-revving capability and dual intake systems.
commonFailurePoints: VQ35HR light throttle misfire 3000-3200rpm; gallery gasket failure causing oil leaks

items.title: Nissan Skyline 3.8L Petrol Engines

items.title: Nissan Skyline Petrol Engines
items.description: All Nissan Skyline engines are petrol; the RB, VQ and VR families cover turbo straight-6 and V6 configurations across R32-R35 generations.
items.descriptor: All Nissan Skyline engines are petrol; the RB, VQ and VR families cover turbo straight-6 and V6 configurations across R32-R35 generations.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Skyline Petrol Engine Families
families: RB20DET - Entry-level 2.0L turbo for R32 GTS-t, reliable but CAS-prone
RB25DET/RB25DET NEO - 2.5L turbo for GT-T models, NEO adds VVT and strength
RB26DETT - Legendary 2.6L twin-turbo GT-R engine, oil pump drive weakness on early units
VQ35DE/VQ35HR - 3.5L V6 for V35/V36 350GT, HR variant has high-rev focus
VR38DETT - 3.8L twin-turbo V6 for R35 GT-R, hand-built performance flagship
knownFor: RB26DETT ceramic turbo failure above 0.9-1.0 bar boost
RB25DET CAS timing failure causing misfire under load
VQ35HR light throttle misfire between 3000-3200rpm
Strong aftermarket support for all RB series engines

engineYears.h2: Nissan Skyline Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Skyline production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The R32 Skyline launched with the legendary RB26DETT in GT-R form and RB20DET in GTS-t, defining JDM performance culture.
keyChanges: Introduction of RB26DETT twin-turbo straight-6 for GT-R
RB20DET 2.0L turbo for entry-level GTS-t models
ATTESA E-TS all-wheel drive system debut on GT-R
First generation with widespread UK import presence
knownFor: RB26DETT oil pump drive collar weakness on early R32 units
Ceramic turbo wheels prone to failure above 1.0 bar boost
Lightweight chassis with excellent handling balance
Strong aftermarket support and tuning potential
years.cta: Get quotes for 1989-1994 Nissan Skyline replacement engines
years.ticker: R32 GT-R engine replacement • RB26 oil pump failure fix • R32 ceramic turbo upgrade

years.description: The R33 refined the RB platform with improved oil pump drive design and introduced the RB25DET for GT-T models.
keyChanges: RB26DETT received improved oil pump drive over R32
RB25DET 2.5L single-turbo introduced for GT-T variants
Longer wheelbase and revised suspension geometry
Increased UK registration numbers via official imports
knownFor: RB25DET CAS failure causing misfire at 2500-3500rpm
Improved RB26 oil pump reliability over R32 (but still requires collar fix for track use)
Heavier chassis than R32 but more refined ride quality
Popular choice for drift builds due to RB25DET torque characteristics
years.ticker: R33 GT-T engine replacement • RB25 CAS timing fix • R33 GT-R oil pump upgrade

years.description: The R34 represented the RB platform's peak with the NEO-spec RB25DET and best-factory RB26DETT oil pump design.
keyChanges: RB25DET NEO introduced with VVT and stronger bottom end for GT-T
RB26DETT received best-factory oil pump drive design
Revised aerodynamics and electronics package
Final generation before Infiniti rebadging for global markets
mainEngines: RB25DET NEO - 2.5L petrol single-turbo with VVT (GT-T)
knownFor: RB25DET NEO VVT solenoid issues on higher-mileage units
RB26DETT ceramic turbo failure remains risk if boost increased
Most desirable generation for collectors and performance builds
Strongest factory RB26 oil pump design (but collar fix still recommended for track)
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2002 Nissan Skyline replacement engines
years.ticker: R34 GT-T engine replacement • RB25 NEO VVT fix • R34 GT-R forged build cost

years.description: The V35 marked the shift from RB straight-6 to VQ V6 power, with the 350GT offering refined performance via the VQ35DE.
keyChanges: Introduction of VQ35DE 3.5L V6 replacing RB series
Shift to rear-wheel drive platform shared with Infiniti G35
More luxury-focused interior and equipment levels
First Skyline generation officially sold in some European markets
knownFor: VQ35DE oil consumption issues on higher-mileage units
Gallery gasket failure causing oil leaks
Smooth, refined power delivery vs turbo RB character
Popular choice for daily-driven Skyline enthusiasts

years.description: The V36 introduced the high-revving VQ35HR with dual intake systems and 311bhp output for enhanced responsiveness.
keyChanges: VQ35HR replaced VQ35DE with high-rev focus and dual intake
Revised electronics and driver aids package
Updated styling and interior refinement
Final generation before R35 GT-R became standalone model
mainEngines: VQ35HR - 3.5L petrol V6 with high-rev focus (350GT)
knownFor: VQ35HR light throttle misfire between 3000-3200rpm (throttle body/MAF related)
Gallery gasket failure remains a known issue
Excellent high-revving character and throttle response
Popular for owners seeking refined daily performance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2014 Nissan Skyline replacement engines
years.ticker: V36 misfire fix • VQ35HR throttle body clean • V36 engine replacement cost

years.preview: Present - R35 GT-R Standalone Performance Flagship
years.description: The R35 GT-R became a standalone model with the hand-built VR38DETT twin-turbo V6, representing Nissan's modern performance pinnacle.
keyChanges: Introduction of VR38DETT 3.8L twin-turbo V6, hand-built by Takumi technicians
Advanced ATTESA E-TS all-wheel drive with multiple modes
Dual-clutch transmission and sophisticated electronics package
Continuous evolution with NISMO and Premium variants
knownFor: Turbo actuator failure causing boost control issues on early units
High-pressure fuel pump wear on pre-2012 units
Continuous factory updates addressing known issues
Premium pricing for replacement due to hand-built nature and complexity
years.cta: Compare Nissan Skyline R35 GT-R engine prices
years.ticker: R35 GT-R engine replacement • VR38 actuator fix • R35 forged build cost

faq.h2: Nissan Skyline Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Nissan Skyline engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Nissan Skyline engine replacement costs vary significantly by engine code: RB20DET £1,500-£2,500 used, RB25DET £2,000-£6,000, RB26DETT £5,000-£25,000 depending on condition and build specification.
keyPoints: RB26DETT (GT-R): £5,000-£10,000 used, £10,000-£25,000 rebuilt/forged
VQ35HR (350GT): £2,500-£5,500 rebuilt typical range
VR38DETT (R35 GT-R): £15,000-£25,000 rebuilt due to hand-built nature
items.cta: Compare Nissan Skyline engine replacement prices for your variant

items.question: Is the RB26DETT engine reliable in the Nissan Skyline GT-R?
items.answer: The RB26DETT is robust when maintained but has known weaknesses: oil pump drive failure on early R32/R33 units and ceramic turbo wheel failure if boost exceeds 1.0 bar.
keyPoints: Oil pump drive collar fix essential for early R32/R33 GT-Rs
Factory ceramic turbos fail if boost increased beyond stock levels
Timing belt must be replaced every 100,000km (interference engine)
Strong bottom end supports ~600bhp on good tune with proper maintenance
Forged internals recommended for serious performance builds
items.cta: Get quotes for RB26DETT Nissan Skyline engine replacement with upgraded oil pump

items.question: What causes the Nissan Skyline RB25DET to misfire under boost?
items.answer: RB25DET misfire under load is most commonly caused by crank angle sensor (CAS) failure, followed by coil pack issues or boost leaks affecting air/fuel ratio.
keyPoints: CAS failure causes timing errors manifesting as misfire at 2500-3500rpm
Individual coil packs fail causing cylinder-specific misfire
Boost leaks or MAF sensor issues can mimic CAS failure symptoms
Oil pressure sensor failure may give false low readings causing unnecessary concern
OEM or upgraded aftermarket CAS units recommended for replacement
items.cta: Compare RB25DET Nissan Skyline engine prices after CAS diagnosis

items.question: Can I fit an RB26DETT engine into a non-GT-R Nissan Skyline?
items.answer: RB26DETT swaps into non-GT-R Skylines are possible but require significant custom work including ECU, wiring harness, transmission and cooling system modifications.
keyPoints: Engine mounts, transmission tunnel and exhaust require modification
ATTESA E-TS AWD system integration is complex and costly
ECU and wiring harness must be adapted or replaced with standalone management
Cooling system upgrades essential due to RB26's higher heat output
Professional JDM specialist fitting strongly recommended for safety and reliability
items.cta: Enquire about RB26DETT Nissan Skyline engine swap supply & fit services

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Nissan Skyline engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Nissan Skyline engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points can last 100,000+ miles with regular maintenance and appropriate use.
keyPoints: Rebuilt RB25DET with new CAS/coils typically lasts 80k-120k miles
RB26DETT with crank collar fix and steel turbos supports high-mileage reliability
VQ35HR with updated gallery gaskets and throttle body service extends lifespan
Regular oil changes with correct specification critical for longevity
Forged builds support higher power but require more frequent maintenance
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Skyline rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: What's the difference between RB25DET and RB25DET NEO engines?
items.answer: The RB25DET NEO features variable valve timing (VVT), stronger connecting rods, improved oil pump design and 276bhp output versus the non-NEO's 250bhp and simpler design.
keyPoints: NEO variant exclusive to R34 GT-T and 25GT Turbo models
VVT system improves low-end torque and high-RPM power delivery
Stronger bottom end supports higher power outputs safely
NEO oil pump design more robust than earlier RB25 units
NEO commands premium pricing due to performance advantages
items.cta: Compare RB25DET NEO Nissan Skyline engine prices for R34 models

items.question: Why does my V36 Skyline 350GT misfire at 3000rpm on light throttle?
items.answer: VQ35HR misfire at 3000-3200rpm on light throttle is typically caused by throttle body carbon buildup or MAF sensor contamination creating a lean condition.
keyPoints: Carbon buildup in throttle body disrupts airflow at part-throttle
Dirty MAF sensor sends incorrect air mass data to ECU
VVT solenoid issues can contribute to timing-related misfire
ECU adaptation reset often required after cleaning components
Professional tuning can resolve persistent misfire after mechanical fixes
items.cta: Get quotes for VQ35HR Nissan Skyline engine replacement if misfire persists

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding a Nissan Skyline engine or buying used?
items.answer: Rebuilding is worthwhile for GT-R models and clean-chassis GT-T cars due to high values; used engines suit budget GTS-t or high-mileage GT-T replacements.
keyPoints: GT-R (R32/R33/R34) values justify rebuilt/forged investment
GT-T with clean chassis benefits from rebuilt RB25DET with warranty
GTS-t or rust-affected cars may not justify rebuild costs
Used engines require compression/oil pressure verification before purchase
Forged builds hold value well for performance-focused owners
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Nissan Skyline engine prices for your situation

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Nissan Skyline engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned and rebuilt Nissan Skyline engines typically include 6-12 months unlimited mileage warranty; used engines usually carry 3-6 months coverage.
keyPoints: 12-month warranty standard on professionally rebuilt units
Used/breaker engines typically offer 3-6 months limited coverage
Extended warranty available for forged performance builds
Warranty terms vary by supplier - always confirm exact coverage
Supply & fit installations may include additional labour warranty
items.cta: Find Nissan Skyline engine suppliers offering 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: How do I identify my Nissan Skyline engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the block near the bellhousing; RB20DET, RB25DET, RB26DETT, VQ35HR and VR38DETT are the primary Skyline codes.
keyPoints: Locate stamp on engine block near transmission bellhousing
Check VIN plate for model code correlating to engine type
R32/R33/R34: RB20DET (GTS-t), RB25DET (GT-T), RB26DETT (GT-R)
V35/V36: VQ35DE/HR (350GT); R35: VR38DETT (GT-R only)
Enter registration on EngineMarket for instant DVLA-verified identification
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your exact Nissan Skyline engine code instantly
